root_cause: DrugCentral (University of New Mexico) has no public REST API. The site is a Django web application with HTML-only pages. All tested /api/ endpoint patterns return 404. The '/OpenAPI' page references a Pharos API iframe (pharos-api.newdrugtargets.org) but that server has an expired SSL certificate. The TRAPI wrapper (automat.transltr.io/drugcentral) returns empty results for all tested CURIE identifiers. The BioThings repoDB API (biothings.ncats.io/repodb) returns 503.
workaround: OpenFDA Drugs@FDA API (api.fda.gov/drug/drugsfda.json) provides FDA drug approval data including submission history, sponsor names, application numbers, and approved products. Existing OpenFDA tools (openfda_tools.json, openfda_label_tools.json) cover labels, adverse events, and NDC. New OpenFDA_search_drug_approvals, OpenFDA_get_approval_history, OpenFDA_get_approved_products tools added.
investigation_notes: drugcentral.org/drugcard/84 returns HTML (aspirin page) but no JSON endpoint
drugcentral.org/OpenAPI page embeds iframe from pharos-api.newdrugtargets.org but SSL cert expired
TRAPI wrapper automat.transltr.io/drugcentral has metadata but returns empty for PUBCHEM.COMPOUND:2244, CHEBI:15365, DRUGBANK:DB00945, DrugCentral:84, UNII:R16CO5Y76E
BioThings repoDB API at biothings.ncats.io/repodb returns 503 Service Unavailable
DrugCentral data is available as PostgreSQL dump download (unmtid-shinyapps.net/download/DrugCentral/) but not via REST API
SmartAPI registry shows only TRAPI wrapper, no direct DrugCentral REST API registered
